what: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc --core array stack: wires (file-backed, memory-mapped storage), (GGUF model files fmalloc tensors) (vendored GGML compute runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels) LLM inference — quantized weights stay GGUF encoding, memory-mapped disk, never decoded double compute path. Two things live : codec-aware matmul backend: dense %*% quantized_fmalloc_tensor runs natively quantized space GGML (registered Rfmalloc’s backend registry selected load); llama-architecture inference engine: GGUF loader, forward pass, KV cache, byte-level generation.

what: Quantized products, zero-copy (runs at render time)
title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
code: library(Rllm)   # registers + selects the ggml backend #> Rllm: ggml quantized matmul backend registered and active for Rfmalloc typed tensors (disable with rllm_use_ggml(FALSE)).  rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) set.seed(1) W  <- matrix(rnorm(512 * 8, sd = 0.4), nrow = 512) Wq <- rllm_quantize_tensor(W, "q4_k", runtime = rt)  # 4.5 bits/weight, mmap'd X  <- matrix(rnorm(4 * 512), nrow = 4) Y  <- X %*% Wq                                       # GGML SIMD, no f64 decode sqrt(sum((Y - X %*% W)^2) / sum((X %*% W)^2))        # q4_k-accurate #> [1] 0.09047354

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: model /O boundary token ids raw bytes — never character vectors core. rllm_encode()/rllm_decode() edge codecs built GGUF’s byte-level BPE metadata (external tokenizer); rawToChar() caller’s interpretation. boundary serves byte-level, vision, pure-codec models later. transcript real recorded run (SmolLM2-135M Q4_K_M, 30 layers, grouped-query attention, q5_0-dominant quantization mix): ~16 tokens/s CPU decode KV cache, 8.5x cache-less re-forwards, identical outputs. engine : rllm_gguf_model() reads hyperparameters GGUF metadata imports 2-d weights natively (still quantized) fmalloc-backed storage; forward pass points GGML tensors payloads zero-copy. rllm_forward() assembles GGML graph (RMSNorm, RoPE, causal self-attention GQA, SwiGLU) computes GGML CPU backend ’s C-callables — Rllm links GGML . rllm_kv_cache() allocates per-layer cache slabs, plain R memory fmalloc-backed (cache disk citizen: file-backed, evictable, open quantized-cache codec later). rllm_generate() prefills cache decodes greedily one token per step, EOS-aware.
code: model <- rllm_gguf_model("SmolLM2-135M.Q4_K_M.gguf") model #> <rllm_model llama: 30 layers, n_embd 576, 9/3 heads, n_ff 1536, #>  vocab 49152; 272 tensors (q5_0/f32/q4_k/q8_0/q6_k)>  gen <- rllm_generate(model, charToRaw(     "The capital of France is Paris. The capital of Germany is"), n_new = 16L) rawToChar(gen$raw) #> [1] " Berlin. The capital of Italy is Rome. The capital of Spain is Madrid."

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: Every layer pinned independent reference: forward pass matches pure-R implementation arithmetic ~1e-6 relative real-model weights (f32-twin roundtrip) < 1e-4 hermetic synthetic models (MHA GQA), causality probes; incremental (KV-cache) logits equal whole-batch logits every position; codec decoders bit-identical GGML’s type-traits to_float (Rggml_dequantize), cross-validation caught real upstream gguf-tools Q4_K bug; rllm_decode(rllm_encode(x)) always x. hermetic tests write synthetic GGUF models test time ’s writer; opt-smoke test (RLLM_TEST_GGUF=<path llama-arch GGUF>) exercises real files.

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors
text: Today, inference runs GGML CPU backend: runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels (AVX2 x86, NEON aarch64, CPUID-selected runtime — non-portable flags recorded) dense products offloadable R’s BLAS. CUDA next backend (roadmap — implemented yet). plan follows pattern ’s BLAS backend: ’s configure autodetect nvcc — absent, nothing changes (CRAN/CI builds unaffected); present, GGML’s CUDA backend compiled exposed Rggml_backend_cuda_init C-callable, Rllm gains backend switch forward/generate. target validated: identical model quantization decode ~455 tokens/s (~28x CPU stack) RTX 5050 via upstream GGML CUDA. Vulkan remains long-term roadmap non-NVIDIA GPUs.

title: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc Tensors

what: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc ecosystem. registers Rggml (vendored GGML build runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels) codec-aware matrix-multiply backend Rfmalloc, products file-backed quantized tensors run natively quantized space rather decoded double first. Together Rgguf (exposes GGUF model weights Rfmalloc-backed tensors) lets larger--memory model's linear layers multiply GGML's SIMD-accelerated dot kernels, zero-copy memory-mapped payload.

title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Loading package registers selects "ggml" backend; toggle rllm_use_ggml(). Build quantized tensors rllm_quantize_tensor().

title: Rllm: Native Quantized Matrix Products and LLM Inference over Rfmalloc — Rllm-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com

what: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: ids <-> bytes edge codecs bytes-boundary API, built model's GGUF tokenizer metadata (byte-level BPE, tokenizer.ggml.model == "gpt2"). rllm_decode() maps ids exact bytes stand . rllm_encode() byte-pair-encodes bytes ids using file's merge ranks; applies merges without GPT-2's regex pre-tokenizer, tokenization may occasionally differ llama.cpp's canonical split - every output still valid encoding input (rllm_decode(rllm_encode(x)) always x).

title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: model rllm_model whose GGUF carries byte-level BPE tokenizer. ids Integer vector 0-based token ids. x raw vector (single string, converted charToRaw()).

title: Convert between token ids and raw bytes — rllm_decode
text: rllm_decode(): raw vector. rllm_encode(): integer vector 0-based token ids.

what: Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
title: Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
text: Assembles GGML compute graph llama-architecture forward pass (RMSNorm, RoPE, causal self-attention, SwiGLU feed-forward) model's memory-mapped weights computes GGML CPU backend. Quantized weights contracted natively SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels - never decoded double.

title: Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). tokens Integer vector 0-based token ids (GGUF vocab). cache Optional rllm_kv_cache() incremental decoding.

title: Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
text: numeric matrix logits, dim c(n_vocab, length(tokens)): column scores token following position .

title: Run a transformer forward pass and return the logits — rllm_forward
text: Without cache, graph attends whole token batch causal mask (prompt scoring). rllm_kv_cache(), pass appends new tokens' keys/values cache attends everything cached far, advancing cache$n_past - incremental-decoding path: prefill prompt, feed one token time.

what: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: bytes-/bytes-generation entry point: prefills KV cache prompt (ids raw bytes), decodes one token per step - step single-token rllm_forward() cache, full re-forward. Stops n_new tokens model's EOS token. Decoding greedy default (temperature = 0); set positive temperature temperature-scaled sampling, optionally narrowed top_k /top_p.

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
code: rllm_generate(   model,   prompt,   n_new = 32L,   temperature = 0,   top_k = 0L,   top_p = 1,   seed = NULL,   cache = NULL,   runtime = NULL )

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). prompt Integer vector 0-based token ids, raw vector (encoded rllm_encode(); single string converted via charToRaw() convenience). n_new Maximum number tokens generate. temperature Sampling temperature. 0 (default) greedy/argmax; larger values flatten distribution (diverse). top_k Keep top_k highest-logit tokens sampling (0, default, disables cutoff). Ignored greedy. top_p Nucleus sampling: keep smallest set tokens whose probabilities sum least top_p (1, default, disables ). Ignored greedy. seed Optional integer seed, reproducible sampling. cache Optional rllm_kv_cache() continue ; default fresh cache sized length(prompt) + n_new created. runtime Optional Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime cache slabs (file-backed cache).

title: Generate tokens with a KV cache (greedy or sampled) — rllm_generate
text: list ids (prompt + generated, 0-based), new_ids (generated tokens), raw (generated tokens decoded bytes, NULL model tokenizer metadata).

what: Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
title: Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
text: Reads hyperparameters weights llama-architecture 'GGUF' file rllm_model object usable rllm_forward(). 2-d weight tensors imported Rgguf::gguf_tensor(= "native"): payloads keep GGUF storage density (still q4_k/f32/... encoded) Rfmalloc-backed, memory-mapped storage, forward pass points GGML tensors zero-copy. 1-d norm weights small staged packed f32 buffers.

title: Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
text: path Path GGUF file. runtime Optional Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime weight payloads; NULL uses Rfmalloc's default runtime. rope_mode RoPE flavour: 0 (normal/interleaved, llama) 2 (NEOX-style, e.g. qwen2). Defaults 0.

title: Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
text: object class rllm_model: list hparams (named numeric list), tensors (named list weight payloads), rope_mode.

title: Load a llama-architecture GGUF model for forward passes — rllm_gguf_model
text: loader expects standard llama tensor names (token_embd.weight, blk.<>.attn_q.weight, ..., output_norm.weight) hyperparameter keys (<arch>.block_count, <arch>.embedding_length, ...). Models tied embeddings (output.weight) reuse token_embd.weight output projection.

what: Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
title: Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: Allocates per-layer key/value cache slabs incremental rllm_forward() writes attends . slab raw vector n_ctx * (n_embd / n_head) * n_head_kv f32 values - plain R memory default, Rfmalloc-backed (file-backed, memory-mapped) runtime given, makes cache disk citizen: survives runtime's file pages evictable like fmalloc payload. (quantized cache codec TurboQuant/PolarQuant vein can later replace f32 slabs without touching graph.)

title: Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: model rllm_model rllm_gguf_model(). n_ctx Maximum number positions cache can hold. runtime Optional Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc() runtime slabs.

title: Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: environment class rllm_kv_cache fields k, v (per-layer lists raw vectors), n_ctx, n_past.

title: Create a KV cache for incremental decoding — rllm_kv_cache
text: returned object environment, rllm_forward() can advance n_past reference.

what: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: Encodes dense numeric matrix GGUF quantized block format stores compressed payload Rfmalloc-backed (file-backed, memory-mapped) storage, returning fmalloc_tensor. write-side counterpart Rgguf's gguf_tensor(..., = "native"): resulting tensor keeps quantized storage density , multiplied right-hand operand dense %*% tensor ggml backend active (see rllm_use_ggml()), contracted natively quantized space GGML's SIMD-dispatched kernels without ever decoded double.

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: x numeric matrix quantize. dtype Target quantized codec, one "q4_0", "q4_1", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q8_0", "q2_k", "q3_k", "q4_k" (default), "q5_k", "q6_k". runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle (see Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc()); NULL, Rfmalloc's default runtime used.

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: fmalloc_tensor given dtype dim(x).

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
text: number rows x quantized (per-row) dimension must multiple codec's block size: 256 K-quants ("q2_k", "q3_k", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q6_k") 32 "q4_0", "q4_1", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q8_0".

title: Quantize a matrix into an Rfmalloc-backed quantized tensor — rllm_quantize_tensor
code: rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) set.seed(1) W <- matrix(rnorm(256 * 4, sd = 0.4), nrow = 256)  # 256 must divide nrow Wt <- rllm_quantize_tensor(W, "q4_k", runtime = rt) X <- matrix(rnorm(3 * 256), nrow = 3)              # 3 x 256 Y <- X %*% Wt                                      # native quantized product dim(Y) #> [1] 3 4

what: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Rllm registers Rggml Rfmalloc codec-aware matrix-multiply backend named "ggml" selects load. active, products quantized fmalloc_tensors (types "q4_0", "q4_1", "q8_0", "q2_k", "q4_k", "q6_k") tensor right-hand operand (dense %*% tensor) computed ggml quantized space, contracting weight row GGML's SIMD-dispatched dot kernel, dense operand quantized fly. products (tensor left, non-quantized codecs) declined fall back Rfmalloc's decode--BLAS path, results always correct regardless selected backend.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: enable TRUE (default) select "ggml" backend; FALSE restore Rfmalloc's default BLAS path.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Invisibly, TRUE ggml backend active afterwards. rllm_backend_enabled() returns TRUE ggml backend active Rfmalloc matmul backend.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
text: Selection Rfmalloc-scoped; base R's %*% unaffected.

title: Enable or disable the ggml quantized matmul backend — rllm_use_ggml
code: rllm_backend_enabled() #> [1] TRUE rllm_use_ggml(FALSE)   # fall back to Rfmalloc's BLAS decode path rllm_use_ggml(TRUE)    # re-enable ggml quantized products

title: Rllm 0.1.0 (unreleased)
text: rllm_generate() gained sampling: temperature (0 = greedy, default, existing behaviour unchanged), top_k, top_p (nucleus), seed reproducibility. Greedy stays deterministic; sampled decoding reproducible fixed seed. sampler pure logic logits vector (test_sampling.R). Added incremental decoding KV cache bytes-boundary generation API. rllm_kv_cache() allocates per-layer f32 cache slabs (plain R memory, fmalloc-backed given runtime - cache disk citizen; quantized cache codec can later replace slabs without touching graph). rllm_forward(cache =) appends new keys/values attends everything cached (llama.cpp’s classic layout: K flat, V transposed), advancing n_past reference. rllm_generate() prefills cache decodes greedily one token per step; SmolLM2-135M measures ~16 tok/s, 8.5x full re-forwards, identical outputs. correctness invariant - incremental logits equal whole-batch logits every position - pinned synthetic model plain fmalloc cache backings (test_kv_cache.R). model /O boundary bytes, text: rllm_encode()/ rllm_decode() convert raw bytes token ids using GGUF’s byte-level BPE metadata (tokenizer.ggml.tokens/merges, GPT-2 byte alphabet) - external tokenizer; rawToChar() caller’s interpretation. Encoding applies merge ranks without GPT-2’s regex pre-tokenizer, splits may occasionally differ llama.cpp’s canonical ones always decoding back bytes. Real-model record: “capital France Paris. capital Germany ” continues ” Berlin. capital Italy Rome. capital Spain Madrid.” Validated real model (SmolLM2-135M Q4_K_M, 30 layers, GQA 9:3, 272 tensors q4_k/q5_0/q6_k/q8_0/f32 mix): model’s decoded weights, GGML graph matches pure-R reference forward 1e-06 relative (f32-twin roundtrip written Rgguf’s writer), native quantized path agrees argmax; ~0.19 relative logit deviation Q8_K-activation/quantized-weight arithmetic compounded 30 layers, graph error. opt-smoke test (RLLM_TEST_GGUF=<path>, test_real_model.R) exercises loader graph real files without affecting CI/CRAN. Registered GGML-backed Rfmalloc codecs GGUF quantized types Rgguf’s vendored gguflib decode - q5_0, q5_1, q3_k, q5_k (real Q4_K_M model files full q5_0 tensors). decoder GGML’s reference to_float via Rggml_dequantize, codecs consistent--construction compute path; block geometry taken vendored GGML registration. rllm_quantize_tensor() typed-GEMM bridge accept new types . Added llama-architecture forward pass: rllm_gguf_model() loads GGUF model’s hyperparameters weights (2-d tensors imported natively - still q4_k/f32/… encoded - fmalloc-backed, memory-mapped storage), rllm_forward() assembles GGML compute graph (RMSNorm, RoPE, causal self-attention grouped-query support, SwiGLU) Rggml’s graph-op C-callables weights zero-copy, computing logits every position token batch GGML CPU backend. Quantized weights contracted SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels without ever decoded double. KV cache yet (whole-batch causal attention: prompt-scoring entry point, incremental generation). Verified pure-R reference implementation arithmetic synthetic GGUF model written test time - logits agree float accumulation error (< 1e-4 relative) multi-head grouped-query configurations, plus causality probes (test_llama_forward.R). Initial release. Rllm composition layer Rfmalloc ecosystem, wiring together Rfmalloc (file-backed storage), Rgguf (GGUF weights fmalloc tensors) Rggml (vendored GGML compute runtime-SIMD-dispatched quantized kernels). Registers Rggml Rfmalloc codec-aware (“typed”) matrix-multiply backend named "ggml", selected load (toggle rllm_use_ggml()). active, product dense %*% quantized_tensor (tensor fmalloc_tensor GGUF quantized codec: q4_0, q4_1, q8_0, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k) computed natively quantized space: Rfmalloc hands raw compressed payload Rllm, points GGML tensor zero-copy contracts weight row GGML’s SIMD-dispatched vec_dot, quantizing dense operand fly - decode double. Orientations codecs ggml path serve (tensor left, non-quantized codecs) declined fall back Rfmalloc’s decode--BLAS path, results always correct regardless selected backend. rllm_quantize_tensor() encodes dense matrix GGUF quantized block format stores payload Rfmalloc-backed storage, returning fmalloc_tensor - write-side counterpart Rgguf’s gguf_tensor(..., = "native").
